rationale: Commit 943cc310 closes both downstream-contract gaps I raised. Bead 1 now requires the workflow schema doc to cover the verdict JSON payload, not just filenames: verdict enum, top-level hard_block, reviewer-authored presentation fields, and the gate-versus-presentation split. Its drift test pins the enum literals, hard_block, filename examples, and non-finding-level wording. Bead 1 also makes the BRIEF stub carry that same contract once, and Bead 5 removes the old ms-panel-run verdict-output block and the ambiguous finding-level hard_block sentence from both skill copies. The remaining Artifact gates text is clearly judgment that sets the top-level flag; tally still reads the binary decision. No new contract ambiguity for spec 111's non-Claude runner.

findings.title: Verdict JSON payload is now part of the documented artifact contract
findings.details: The plan now requires .mindspec/domains/workflow/interfaces.md to document the verdict payload fields, including the APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/REJECT enum, optional top-level hard_block as a sibling of verdict, reviewer-authored fields, and the explicit split between gate-consumed verdict/hard_block and presentation-only fields. TestPanelSchemaDoc_MatchesConstants is expanded to pin the enum literals, hard_block, and non-finding-level wording against doc drift.

findings.title: BRIEF and skill instructions now have one non-conflicting verdict contract
findings.details: The machine-managed BRIEF header's Your job block is specified as the single verdict JSON instruction and names top-level hard_block. Bead 5 removes the skill-authored verdict-output block and the old 'artifact-gate finding may set hard_block' phrasing from both plugins and .claude copies, while retaining Artifact gates only as judgment that sets the flag the binary tally reads.
